data.narrative:
Deep Fission operates in the nuclear energy sector at the public stage, having raised $110M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private entities, including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private peers total, with insufficient overlap when filtered by stage and valuation tier. Specifically, the narrow peer set identified only 2 same-stage comparables, 0 adjacent-stage peers, and 0 similar-tier companies—below the minimum threshold of 3 required for reliable median estimation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given the specialized capital intensity and regulatory environment of nuclear energy development.

Without a sufficiently sized comparable cohort meeting standard filtering criteria, traditional triangulation methods cannot produce a defensible valuation range. Further analysis would require either: broadening the peer definition to include earlier or later-stage nuclear companies with disclosed valuations; obtaining primary pricing data from recent financings within Deep Fission's immediate peer group; or applying DCF-derived benchmarks from mature nuclear operators. The $110M in total capital raised establishes a floor, but does not indicate current fair value absent additional methodological inputs.
